perturbation.original_text: The term “Confidential Information” shall include all Hydranautics’ trade secrets and information that conc erns the confidential business or affairs of Hydranautics and Nitto including without limitation, financial information and reports, business plans, design and construction plans, manufacturing processes, customer lists, employee data, forecasts, strategies, and all other proprietary business information.
perturbation.changed_text: Visitor agrees he/she will not remove any document, equipment or other materials from the premises without Hydranautics’ written consent. 4. Visitor agrees and acknowledges that in the course of, or incident to, the tour of Hydranautics’ facilities, Hydranutics may provide to, or Visitor will otherwise become exposed to Hydranautics’ Confidential Information. 1. The term “Confidential Information” shall include all Hydranautics’ trade secrets and information that conc erns the confidential business or affairs of Hydranautics and Nitto including without limitation, financial information and reports, business plans, design and construction plans, manufacturing processes, customer lists, employee data, forecasts, strategies, and all other proprietary business information.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the definition of "Confidential Information" to after the clause that states visitor may be exposed to it creates confusion about what the Confidential Information encompasses and undermines the agreement's clarity. It is less obvious to the visitor what data is considered confidential.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 1644 dictates that the words of a contract are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense, unless used by the parties in a technical sense, or a special meaning is given to them by usage, in which case the latter is followed. By moving the definition of "Confidential Information" to after the clause stating that the visitor will be exposed to it, you force the visitor to keep in mind, or go searching through the document for what would be considered Confidential Information. This would require the visitor to interpret the definition of "Confidential Information" without being properly defined.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1644 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS [1635 - 1663] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) 1644. The words of a contract are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense, rather than according to their strict legal meaning; unless used by the parties in a technical sense, or unless a special meaning is given to them by usage, in which case the latter must be followed. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a structural flaw by delaying the definition of 'Confidential Information' until after a clause stating that the visitor may be exposed to it. This violates California Civil Code § 1644, as cited in the Law Explanation, because the visitor must interpret the definition of 'Confidential Information' without it being properly defined beforehand. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that 'The words of a contract are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense...unless a special meaning is given to them by usage, in which case the latter must be followed.' Here, the ordinary sense cannot be understood until after reading the definition, which contradicts the intent of the law.
